items.title: 'Most people didn't know': MPs share mental health struggles

children.content: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

gallery.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

items.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change

children.content: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

image.altText: A penguin chicken stands in the foreground. Camera crews are in the distance.

items.title: Call for 'collective action' to address gendered violence in Tasmania

children.content: A new platform, dubbed Justice For Her, is centred on making small, actionable pledges to help people overcome the overwhelming statistics on gendered violence, which often lead to inaction.

image.altText: Sally Haynes and Carolyn Self launching "Justice for Her" 2026-04-08 10:04:00

items.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses

children.content: The temporary ability to catch public transport for free is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others are using it for new experiences.

image.altText: A man in a 'PROTECT TAKAYNA' shirt stands in front of a bus going to Devonport.

items.title: Grace Tame Foundation announces closure

children.content: The Grace Tame Foundation, established by the Tasmanian-born survivor of child sexual abuse, has announced it is closing because securing funding is becoming "increasingly challenging".

image.altText: A woman wearing a grey cardigan with her hair in a pony tail looks seriously at the camera in a building corridor.

items.title: Super fund members claim they were 'misled' after being forced to sell assets

children.content: A superannuation investment option spruiked as offering "choice and flexibility" has been shut down without proper notification, leaving members less than two months to sell all their assets.

gallery.altText: Lauren sits on the couch with laptop in the lap, her hair in a high bun

thumbnail.altText: Lauren sits on the couch with laptop in the lap, her hair in a high bun

items.title: Construction businesses vulnerable to supply chain shock, experts warn

children.content: Contractors, business owners and economists are warning that thousands of construction industry businesses could go bust as a result of supply chain disruptions caused by the Iran War, unless the government intervenes.

image.altText: Housing construction

items.title: The NDIS's image has taken a beating before — but not like this

children.content: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

avatar.alt: A vision impaired woman with long hair in a blue dress holding a cane

items.title: Indians speak out after Little India announcement met with racism

children.content: Social media posts about the announcement of a Little India precinct in Melbourne are flooded with racist comments, leaving some Indians with mixed feelings about the plan.

gallery.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

thumbnail.altText: Purva wearing a pink sari while speaking on stage at a panel

items.title: Queues expected as Europe brings in tighter border measures

children.content: Australians travelling to Europe will be fingerprinted and face-scanned at the border from today, as authorities activate a sweeping new digital system designed to track who enters and leaves the continent.

image.altText: A man operates an automated EES kiosk at a european border

items.title: Warning projects at risk of delay as Queensland suburbs build upwards

children.content: South-east Queensland councils are building upwards in a bid to house hundreds of thousands of new residents in the coming years.

image.altText: A rendered image of a street with townhouses and apartments

items.title: Animals on wildlife market more likely to carry human-infecting disease, report finds

children.content: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

gallery.altText: four birds sit on a ledge inside a market

thumbnail.altText: four birds sit on a ledge inside a market

items.title: Crows get home against Blues in the wet in Gather Round opener

children.content: The Adelaide Crows send the fans home happy after ending its losing streak by beating Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval to start Gather Round. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide AFL player tries to kick the ball near the boundary as he is tackled by a Carlton player in torrential rain.

items.title: Bulldogs stun misfiring Panthers in big boilover victory

children.content: The Penrith Panthers unbeaten start to the NRL season has been ended in comprehensive fashion by the Bulldogs at Stadium Australia.

image.altText: Lachlan Galvin celebrates with teammates

items.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town

children.content: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

items.title: How one of the world's best ballet dancers ended up in Australia

children.content: The Australian Ballet helped put David Hallberg back together after a grave injury. He now returns that favour and leads our national ballet company with a new verve and energy.

image.altText: David Hallberg stands hands in pockets on stage in all black, with a pair of ballet dancers posing on both sides of him

items.title: How to get the most out of your bath towels — and when to replace them

children.content: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

image.altText: A boys hands holding a pile of well-worn bath towels

items.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between fuel and food

children.content: The high cost of fuel is making it even more difficult for people living in their cars to afford food, electricity and transport.

articles.title: Diesel prices hit new post-war peak, despite excise cuts

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

articles.title: How long will it take petrol prices to get back to pre-Iran war levels, if at all?

children.content: Iran's promise to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after striking a temporary agreement with the US saw the cost of oil fall sharply. But how quickly are we likely to see relief at the bowser in Australia? Analysts say it could be weeks or months.

image.altText: Fuel bowser with a big truck in the background.

articles.title: How is the war in Iran impacting you or your business? Share with us

children.content: As fuel costs soar and the global economy reacts to a shortage of oil supplies, we need your help in identifying the impacts here in Australia. Whether it's the cost of your work commute or supply shortages affecting your business, share it with us.

image.altText: A row of petrol pumps.

children.content: His career began as a substitute for Mark Bosnich almost 20 years ago, now Andrew Redmayne is joining the A-League's exclusive 300 club.

items.title: Families demand refunds for Easter carnival which left 'kids in tears'

children.content: A Melbourne council is investigating an Easter event held at the Werribee racecourse after it was criticised by attendees, who said it felt unsafe, underwhelming and poorly organised.

image.altText: A large number of children and adults search through hay, holding baskets on a sunny day.

items.title: Track closures continue in Queensland. Here's what you need to know

children.content: After a week of "chaotic" disruptions, passengers should prepare for further impacts with the extended April track closures to get into full swing.

image.altText: A train at a platform at Roma Street Station in Brisbane

items.title: King Charles writes 'very different' open letter after NT flood disaster

children.content: King Charles has written an open letter to Northern Territory residents following recent devastating flooding, saying the "increasingly catastrophic effects of climate change are surely a siren-call to the world for more urgent action".

gallery.altText: a close up of king charles, wrinkled and red face with a receding grey hairline
An one-page letter from King Charles to NT residents, acknowledging the devastation of widespread flooding.
Brown water spills across streets in a small community, shown from above.

thumbnail.altText: a close up of king charles, wrinkled and red face with a receding grey hairline

items.title: Gang rapist on the run after escaping custody at Sydney hospital

children.content: A man convicted of gang raping a 14-year-old girl in a Western Sydney park has escaped from custody while at a hospital. Follow live.

image.altText: A composite image of a man's face and the same man running across a road.

items.title: High Court refuses to hear Bruce Lehrmann's appeal

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that found, on the balance of probabilities, he did rape Brittany Higgins.

image.altText: Bruce walks outside, flanked by others, wearing a blue suit, white shirt and pink tie and glasses.

items.title: Post-ceasefire attacks in Lebanon spark fears war would get 'much worse'

children.content: As a shaky ceasefire struggles to get under way, deadly Israeli strikes on Lebanon risk worsening the Middle East conflict.

image.altText: thick black smoke rising from buildings

items.title: Is Hormuz open or closed? Shiptracking data reveals true picture

children.content: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has remained at an effective standstill in the 24 hours since Iran conditionally lifted its blockade on the critical shipping lane, amid a fragile ceasefire with the US and Israel.

image.altText: A satellite image of hundreds of ships floating in the ocean.

items.title: WA Police launch probe after video shows officer shoving woman to ground during arrest

children.content: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

items.title: China's 26 phone calls that brought on a US-Iran ceasefire

children.content: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi says he held 26 phone calls with his counterparts in Iran, Israel, Russia and Gulf countries before the ceasefire was reached.

image.altText: A man holds up his index finger while talking.

items.title: She hasn't had an injury but $10m horse will never race, heading to retirement instead

children.content: An owner says she has "no regrets" after announcing that the first filly from champion race mare Winx will never race.
